title: Robustly Garlicky Algerian Seafood Tagine
description: A traditional Algerian dish with a garlicky twist, featuring seafood stock and bonito flakes, cooked to perfection in a comal vessel. This recipe is a must try for seafood lovers.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large comal vessel over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the shrimp, mussels, and clams, and cook for 2 minutes, until the seafood starts to open.
Add the seafood stock, bonito flakes, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10 minutes, until the seafood is cooked through.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, the tagine is cooked in a clay vessel with a conical lid, but a comal vessel can be used as a substitute.
Make sure to cook the seafood to an internal temperature of at least 145 degrees Fahrenheit to ensure food safety.
